summary: Complete backend + frontend testing of TheDayLaborers MVP. Fixed a critical job posting bug (missing trailing slash). All major user flows work: landing page, auth, dashboards, admin, subscription, profile, theme toggle, navigation.

minor.issue: Returns 307 redirect. FastAPI requires trailing slash: GET /api/jobs/. Fixed in frontend.
Returns 307 redirect. POST doesn't follow redirects. Fixed by adding trailing slash in ContractorDashboard.jsx.
Returns 403 instead of 401 (minor HTTP semantics, FastAPI behavior). Not a real issue.

ui_bugs.issue: Pie chart disappears when switching to light mode (theme toggle). Chart renders correctly in dark mode only.

issues: Uses browser's native datetime-local picker instead of a custom styled component. Minor UX inconsistency.

bugs_fixed.issue: Missing trailing slash on POST /api/jobs and GET /api/jobs causing 307 redirect → job posting failed
bugs_fixed.fix: Changed ${API}/jobs to ${API}/jobs/ for both GET and POST calls

bugs_fixed.issue: Missing trailing slash on GET /api/jobs causing 307 redirect → crew job list failed

action_items: Fix AdminDashboard pie chart not rendering in light mode
Consider using a custom datetime picker instead of browser's native one for consistency
critical_code_review_comments: Missing trailing slashes on job API URLs (fixed) - FastAPI strict URL matching caused 307 redirects on POST
Admin dashboard chart only renders in dark mode - likely a recharts/canvas color issue in light theme

success_rate.backend: 94% (17/18 tests pass, minor 403 vs 401 semantic issue)
success_rate.frontend: 90% (all core flows work, pie chart light-mode bug)

seed_data_creation: Created test users and jobs during testing. TEST_ prefixed users in DB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Core flows all tested and working. Critical bug fixed: trailing slash on /api/jobs endpoints in ContractorDashboard.jsx and CrewDashboard.jsx. Remaining issue: Admin dashboard pie chart not rendering in light mode. Backend tests are in /app/backend/tests/test_api.py.
rca_of_issue: Root cause of job posting failure: FastAPI routes defined as POST '/'' under prefix '/api/jobs' creates route '/api/jobs/' (with trailing slash). Frontend called '/api/jobs' (no slash). FastAPI returned 307 Temporary Redirect. HTTP clients do not resend POST bodies when following redirects (converts to GET), causing 404/method-not-allowed. Fix: add trailing slash to all bare /jobs calls in frontend.
